title: Decide on name changes
description: Discuss whether either spouse will change their last name, hyphenate, or keep their name. This decision affects every document update that follows, so settle it first.

title: Update your Social Security card
description: If changing your name, file Form SS-5 with the Social Security Administration. This must be done before updating your driver's license, passport, or bank accounts — everything keys off your SSA record.

title: Update your driver's license
description: After your Social Security card reflects the new name, visit the DMV to update your driver's license. Bring your marriage certificate and updated Social Security card.

title: Update your passport
description: If your passport was issued less than a year ago, you may qualify for a free name change. Otherwise, you'll need to renew. Do this before any planned travel under your new name.

title: Obtain certified copies of your marriage certificate
description: Order several certified copies from your county clerk — you'll need them for name changes, insurance updates, and beneficiary forms. Digital copies are not accepted for most official processes.

title: Update or create your will
description: Marriage may invalidate a previous will depending on your state. Create or update your will to reflect your new spouse as heir, and name each other as executor if desired.

title: Set up powers of attorney
description: Create durable power of attorney and healthcare proxy documents so your spouse can make financial and medical decisions on your behalf if you're incapacitated.

title: Discuss and merge your finances
description: Decide whether to combine accounts, keep them separate, or use a hybrid approach. Align on a budget, savings goals, and how to handle shared expenses. This conversation prevents most money conflicts.

title: Update bank account names and details
description: If you changed your name, update it on all bank and investment accounts. If you're adding your spouse to accounts, bring your marriage certificate and both IDs to the bank.

title: Update beneficiaries on all accounts
description: Review and update beneficiary designations on 401(k)s, IRAs, life insurance, bank accounts, and any transfer-on-death accounts. Beneficiary designations override your will, so this is critical.

title: Plan to file taxes jointly
description: Married filing jointly usually results in a lower tax bill, but run the numbers both ways. Adjust your W-4 withholdings with your employer to avoid a surprise at tax time.

title: Review your combined debt and create a payoff plan
description: Be fully transparent about debts — student loans, credit cards, car loans. Build a joint plan to tackle them strategically, prioritizing high-interest balances.

title: Set shared financial goals
description: Discuss short-term and long-term goals together — emergency fund target, home purchase timeline, retirement age, travel plans. Having shared goals makes budgeting feel collaborative, not restrictive.

title: Update health insurance
description: Marriage is a qualifying life event. Compare both spouses' employer plans and choose the best combination of coverage. You have 30 days from the marriage date to make changes.

title: Update auto insurance
description: Combine auto policies for a multi-car discount. Notify your insurer of the marriage — married couples often receive lower rates. Update the name on your policy if applicable.

title: Review and update life insurance
description: Now that someone depends on your income, make sure you have adequate life insurance. Consider term policies for both spouses and name each other as beneficiaries.

title: Update homeowner's or renter's insurance
description: Add your spouse to the policy and update coverage limits to account for combined belongings. If you're moving in together, your insurance needs have likely changed.

title: Update your name with your employer
description: Notify HR to update your name in payroll, email, benefits systems, and company directory. Provide a copy of your marriage certificate and updated Social Security card.

title: Update credit cards and loyalty programs
description: Contact each credit card issuer to update your name. Do the same for airline miles, hotel points, and other loyalty programs. Mismatched names can cause issues at check-in.

title: Update subscriptions and online accounts
description: Update your name and billing info on streaming services, utilities, gym memberships, and any online accounts tied to your old name. A checklist helps — there are more than you think.

title: Update your email signature and professional profiles
description: If you changed your name, update LinkedIn, your email signature, business cards, and professional licenses. Notify clients or professional contacts of the change.

title: Send thank-you notes
description: Send handwritten thank-you notes for wedding gifts within three months of receiving them. Mention the specific gift and how you plan to use it — generic notes feel hollow.

title: Handle wedding vendor wrap-up
description: Confirm all final vendor payments are settled, retrieve your deposit returns, leave reviews for great vendors, and back up your wedding photos and videos to multiple locations.

title: Preserve your wedding dress or suit
description: If you want to preserve your wedding attire, have it professionally cleaned and boxed within a few weeks. Stains set over time and become much harder to remove.

title: Return or exchange duplicate registry gifts
description: Check your registry for duplicates or items you didn't receive but were marked as purchased. Most registries have a generous return window — take advantage of it before it expires.
